title: Introducing Quarters Theory (II) — Intrusion Detector EA
subtitle: Real-Time Price Proximity Detection for Quarter Levels with Alert Deduplication
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: EXTRACT — intrusion proximity check pattern, alert deduplication technique, multi-level simultaneous monitoring, contextual commentary per zone type. No buy/sell signals — pure monitoring.

core_concept.name: Quarter Level Intrusion Detection
core_concept.description: On every tick, check if current bid price is within AlertTolerance distance of any quarter level (major, large quarter, overshoot, small quarter). Fire a contextual alert on first intrusion, suppress re-alerts while price lingers at level (intrusionAlerted flag). Reset flag when price leaves tolerance zone.
core_concept.alert_deduplication: Boolean flag per level: set True on first alert, reset to False only when price moves outside tolerance zone.
core_concept.no_signals: This EA does NOT generate buy/sell signals. It monitors and alerts. Trade decision is made by the trader based on contextual commentary.
core_concept.relevance_to_creviaderiv: The intrusion check pattern is exactly our zone interaction detection. The alert deduplication (intrusionAlerted flag) maps to our 'single signal per zone touch' logic. The contextual commentary per zone type maps to how we classify zone interactions differently for supply vs demand.

intrusion_detection_algorithm.step_1: On each tick: get current bid price
intrusion_detection_algorithm.step_2: Compute active quarter levels from current price and MajorStep
intrusion_detection_algorithm.step_3_check: For each level in [major_lower, major_upper, large_q1, large_q2, large_q3, overshoots, small_quarters]: if abs(price - level) <= alert_tolerance AND NOT already_alerted[level]: fire_alert(level, context_message(level)); already_alerted[level] = True
intrusion_detection_algorithm.step_4_reset: When price moves beyond tolerance: already_alerted[level] = False
intrusion_detection_algorithm.multi_level: All levels checked simultaneously per tick — price can simultaneously intrude multiple zones

AlertTolerance.description: Proximity threshold — approximately 25 pips for standard forex

contextual_commentary.major_support: Key support level. Break below signals range shift.
contextual_commentary.major_resistance: Pivotal resistance. Breakout above may start new range.
contextual_commentary.large_quarter: Decisive break could trigger next 250-PIP move.
contextual_commentary.overshoot: Test of breakout; reversal likely if momentum fails.
contextual_commentary.undershoot: Insufficient bullish force; possible bearish reversal.

steps.name: compute_levels
steps.description: DrawQuarters(): calculate major, large quarter, overshoot, small quarter levels from current price

steps.name: check_intrusions
steps.description: For each level: abs(price - level) <= AlertTolerance AND NOT alerted

steps.name: fire_alert
steps.description: On first intrusion: display contextual message, set alerted flag

steps.name: reset_flag
steps.description: When price leaves tolerance zone: clear alerted flag for that level

steps.name: update_display
steps.description: Update on-chart label table with current price and active zones

key_functions.DrawHorizontalLine: draw_line(name, price, color, width, style) — creates/replaces horizontal chart object, pre-deletes to prevent duplicates
key_functions.DrawQuarters: compute all levels from current price + MajorStep, draw all lines
key_functions.deduplication: intrusionAlerted[level] = True on first hit, reset to False when price exits zone

components_to_build.name: QuarterLevelMonitor
components_to_build.returns: List[Intrusion(level, zone_type, is_new)] where is_new=True on first touch
components_to_build.note: Stateful — tracks which levels have been alerted to enable deduplication.

backtesting.verdict: Live examples only — no formal win rate or statistics
backtesting.caveat: Results shown are discretionary trade examples following alerts. No systematic backtest.

keep: Intrusion proximity check: abs(price - level) <= tolerance — foundational pattern
Alert deduplication via boolean flag per level — prevents signal spam while price lingers
Flag reset when price leaves zone — clean state management
Contextual commentary per zone type — different response for overshoot vs major vs small quarter
Simultaneous multi-level monitoring — all levels checked on every tick
discard: No buy/sell signals — this tool is monitoring-only; we need to extend with signal generation
AlertTolerance = 0.0025 fixed — use ATR-normalized tolerance for cross-instrument use
Forex-only language — 'pips' framing; our version must be instrument-agnostic
improve: ATR-normalized AlertTolerance: tolerance = ATR × 0.5 instead of fixed 0.0025
Add signal classification at overshoot: overshoot intrusion + RSI divergence = high-confidence reversal signal
Track intrusion duration: how many bars has price been in the tolerance zone? Longer = stronger test
Add intrusion strength: depth of penetration beyond zone = momentum measure

creviaderiv_insights.zone_interaction_is_intrusion: Our _is_interacting() check is exactly this intrusion pattern: abs(price - zone_mid) <= zone_half_width. The deduplication flag maps to our single-signal-per-zone-touch logic.
creviaderiv_insights.atr_normalized_tolerance: Their 0.0025 fixed tolerance is naive — 25 pips on EUR/USD but meaningless for Crash 300 at 9000. Our zone_half_width derived from price range is the correct instrument-agnostic version.
creviaderiv_insights.contextual_commentary_maps_to_zone_type: Their 'overshoot = reversal likely if momentum fails' maps directly to our zone type classification: impulsive zone at quarter level = higher reversal probability. The commentary IS our zone quality scoring.
creviaderiv_insights.intrusion_duration_idea: Novel concept: track bars_inside_zone. Short touch (1-2 bars) = quick rejection = stronger signal. Long lingering (5+ bars) = zone being eroded = potentially weaker. Add bars_inside_zone to zone interaction state.
